text: State Budget: Uttar Pradesh FY2024-25. Total: ₹7,37,902 crore. India's largest state budget. Revenue receipts: ₹5,81,000 crore. Key: Roads ₹39,000 cr, Education ₹87,000 cr, Healthcare ₹43,000 cr. Focus: Expressways, industrial corridors.

State Budget: Maharashtra FY2024-25. Total: ₹6,71,010 crore. Revenue receipts: ₹5,10,000 crore. Key: Mumbai infrastructure, Ladki Bahin Yojana (₹46,000 cr), farmer support. Fiscal deficit: 2.5% of GSDP.

State Budget: Tamil Nadu FY2024-25. Total: ₹3,57,000 crore. Revenue receipts: ₹2,75,000 crore. Key: Breakfast scheme, EV manufacturing incentives, healthcare expansion, urban infrastructure. State GSDP: ₹28 lakh crore.

State Budget: Karnataka FY2024-25. Total: ₹3,72,000 crore. Five guarantee schemes cost: ₹52,000 crore. Shakti (free bus), Gruha Jyothi (free electricity), Gruha Lakshmi (₹2000/month), Anna Bhagya, Yuva Nidhi.

State Budget: Gujarat FY2024-25. Total: ₹3,55,000 crore. Key: GIFT City development, semiconductor plant, industrial incentives, diamond and textile sector support. Revenue surplus state. Focus on investment attraction.

State Budget: Bihar FY2024-25. Total: ₹2,62,000 crore. Revenue receipts: ₹1,95,000 crore. Heavy Central transfers dependence. Key: Education ₹42,000 cr, rural roads, flood management. Demand for Special Category State status.
State Budget: Rajasthan FY2024-25. Total: ₹3,05,000 crore. Key: Water conservation ₹28,000 cr (ERCP), tourism, solar energy parks, highway connectivity. Rajasthan largest state by area.

State Budget: West Bengal FY2024-25. Total: ₹3,37,000 crore. Key: Lakshmir Bhandar (₹1000/month to women), Kanyashree (girl education), Duare Sarkar, Swasthya Sathi (health insurance). Strong social welfare focus.
